summary: Iteration 3 admin panel testing complete. Backend: 21/21 pytest passed covering JWT login, verify, list, stats, PATCH read toggle, DELETE, 404s, and public-endpoint regression. Frontend: full admin flow verified — login (wrong→toast, correct→messages page + JWT stored in localStorage), list populated (10+ items), search filters case-insensitive by name/email/text, filter buttons (all/unread/read) work, message detail pane renders sender/email/body + action buttons, auto-mark-read on select decrements unread stat, copy-email button works, mark unread/read toggle, delete flow (window.confirm accepted → item removed, total decrements), refresh picks up newly POSTed messages, session persists across reload, invalid/stale token in localStorage is cleared and shows login page, logout clears token, and public portfolio + theme toggle regression still works. ONE BUG FOUND: Brute-force protection at POST /api/admin/login does NOT trigger 429 as designed — root cause is use of request.client.host which returns the K8s ingress proxy IP (10.231.128.196 / 10.231.128.208) instead of the real client IP, and requests rotate across 2 ingress pods so attempts are split.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout (429 after 5 fails) never triggers behind the K8s ingress. request.client.host returns the ingress proxy IP (e.g. 10.231.128.196 / 10.231.128.208) — requests rotate across 2 ingress pods, so attempts are split into 2 buckets and neither reaches the 5-attempt threshold. Even if unified, all real users would share one rate-limit bucket keyed on the proxy IP — an attacker could then lock out every legitimate user (DoS). Fix: read the first entry of X-Forwarded-For (or X-Real-IP) header when present, e.g. `ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for', '').split(',')[0].strip() or (request.client.host if request.client else 'unknown')`. Verified via curl: 6 consecutive wrong passwords → all returned 401 (expected 429 on 6th); backend log confirms client hosts alternate between 10.231.128.196 and 10.231.128.208.

passed: POST /api/admin/login correct password → 200 {token, expires_at}, JWT is well-formed 3-part token
POST /api/admin/login wrong password → 401 {detail: 'Invalid password'}
GET /api/admin/verify: valid token → 200 {valid:true}; invalid token → 401; missing token → 401
GET /api/admin/messages: unauthenticated → 401; authenticated → 200 list sorted desc by timestamp
GET /api/admin/stats → 200 {total:int, unread:int}
PATCH /api/admin/messages/{id}/read: valid id → returns updated message with read=true, persisted; unknown id → 404; toggling back to read=false works
DELETE /api/admin/messages/{id}: valid id → 200 {status:'deleted', id}; then GET no longer includes id; unknown id → 404
Public regression: GET /api/ → 200 {message:'Portfolio API online'}; POST /api/contact → 200 status=success
Frontend /admin/messages: no token → renders admin-login-page with admin-password-input + admin-login-submit
Wrong password → error toast 'Invalid password' shown, stays on login
Correct password 'changeme-in-env' → transitions to admin-messages-page; localStorage['ac-admin-token'] + 'ac-admin-exp' set
admin-message-list populated after login (10 items), stats admin-stat-total=10 admin-stat-unread=8
Clicking a message shows admin-message-detail with sender name, email, body, and action buttons admin-copy-email / admin-toggle-read / admin-reply / admin-delete
admin-search filters case-insensitively: 'TEST_AdminSeed' → 1 item, 'zzz-not-in-any-message-xyz' → 0 items
Filter buttons admin-filter-all / admin-filter-unread / admin-filter-read filter correctly (8 unread + 2 read → 10 total match)
Selecting an unread message auto-marks read (unread stat decrements)
admin-copy-email clicked without error (clipboard permission granted)
admin-toggle-read toggles state (clicked twice, no error)
admin-refresh reloads list
admin-delete with accepted window.confirm removes item from list; admin-stat-total decrements by 1
New /api/contact POST while signed in appears in list after admin-refresh (total increments 10→11)
admin-logout clears localStorage tokens and returns to login page
Reload with valid token → messages page loads directly (session persistence)
Reload with invalid token in localStorage → login page shown, stale token cleared
Portfolio /: hero-name, theme-toggle, contact-form still render; theme toggle switches dark↔light
action_items: Fix brute-force IP resolution in /app/backend/server.py admin_login handler: use X-Forwarded-For header (first hop) or X-Real-IP instead of request.client.host, so the rate-limit key is the real client IP — not the K8s ingress proxy. Otherwise 5-attempt lockout never fires (proxy IPs rotate) and, if it did fire, would DoS all legitimate users behind the same proxy.
Consider persisting brute-force counters to Redis/Mongo so multiple backend replicas share state (in-memory dict is per-pod).
critical_code_review_comments: Security: `_login_attempts` is an in-memory dict keyed on request.client.host. Behind any reverse proxy (K8s ingress here), this key is the proxy IP, not the client IP → protection is effectively broken (both fails to lock attackers AND risks locking out legitimate users). Must respect X-Forwarded-For.

Security: bcrypt hash is generated fresh on every backend start (line 37-39). This means the hash changes each restart. Functionally fine because comparison is done in-memory, but issued JWTs are still valid across restarts (JWT_SECRET is stable), so no functional break. Just a note.

Minor: /api/contact GET is left publicly accessible for 'backward compatibility' (line 164-172). This exposes all contact messages without any auth. Since the admin panel now provides GET /api/admin/messages, this should be removed or gated behind admin auth in a follow-up.
Minor: DELETE /admin/messages/{id} bypasses response_model — returns raw dict. Consistent with existing style; not a bug.

success_rate.backend: 95% (20/21 requirements — brute-force lockout fails; all pytest cases pass because we intentionally skipped 429 assertion to preserve login for frontend testing)

test_credentials: Admin password: changeme-in-env (from /app/backend/.env ADMIN_PASSWORD). Documented in /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: During testing, several TEST_* contact messages were inserted via POST /api/contact: TEST_Iter3Public, TEST_AdminSeed, TEST_DeleteMe (deleted), TEST_DeleteUI (deleted), TEST_NotifyUI. Also legacy from iteration 2: TEST_Iteration2, TEST_ListCheck, TEST_UIDark, TEST_UILight. These remain in db.contact_messages for retest visibility; main agent can clear via DELETE endpoint if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_api.py (15 admin tests, module-scoped admin_token fixture) + /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_api.py (6 legacy tests). Admin JWT is stored in localStorage['ac-admin-token'] with numeric ms epoch in 'ac-admin-exp'. adminApi.js uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL via components/portfolio/api. Brute-force test is intentionally skipped in pytest to avoid poisoning frontend flows — if you must test lockout, use curl and then `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` to clear counters. Note the in-memory _login_attempts is per-pod, so behavior depends on which K8s pod handles the request. Frontend uses window.confirm for delete — Playwright must attach a `dialog` handler (page.once('dialog', d => d.accept())) BEFORE clicking admin-delete.

rca_of_the_issue.bug: POST /api/admin/login brute-force lockout does not trigger
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: curl 6 consecutive POSTs with wrong password → observed 6× HTTP 401 (expected: 5× 401 + 1× 429).
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: /app/backend/server.py line 205: `ip = request.client.host if request.client else 'unknown'`. Behind K8s ingress, request.client.host is the ingress proxy pod IP (verified 10.231.128.196 and 10.231.128.208 in supervisor backend log). Since the ingress load-balances across 2 pods, wrong-password attempts get keyed to 2 different IPs and neither reaches the MAX_ATTEMPTS=5 threshold. Even with a single ingress pod, this design would incorrectly aggregate all real users' attempts against one shared bucket.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: In admin_login: `ip = (request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') or '').split(',')[0].strip() or (request.client.host if request.client else 'unknown')`. Optionally validate the source of X-Forwarded-For via a trusted-proxy list, or move counters to Redis for multi-pod correctness.
